prose: The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis for simvastatin resolves to negative because the only basis for a call — curated mechanism — is absent: there is no CTD curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registration as a neurodegeneration AOP stressor [E1]. This drives the adversarial_negative tier, a deliberate stress-test of whether the engine can resist a plausible-looking but non-diagnostic activity profile. The compound is genuinely bioactive, with 17 mechanistic assay hits including 2 mitochondrial signals [E2], and it is brain-penetrant [E4]; either feature could superficially suggest concern. Both are treated strictly as corroboration and, in this case, as the imposter signal the engine correctly declines to act upon — assay bioactivity is anti-diagnostic, not decisive, and brain penetrance does not discriminate. Consistent with this, real-world pharmacovigilance shows no parkinsonism signal across 261,343 FAERS reports (ROR 1.07, not elevated) [E3]. The negative call stands.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 17 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 2 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 261343 FAERS reports (ROR 1.07, not elevated).
Brain-penetrant, but penetrance is not a discriminator here.
